# Constitution core (auto-generated — do not edit)

> Stub. This file is regenerated by `/ztn:regen-constitution` (or
> `_system/scripts/gen_constitution_core.py`) from your tier-1 axioms
> and core principles in `0_constitution/{axiom,principle,rule}/`.
>
> On a fresh ZTN clone:
> 1. Run `/ztn:bootstrap` to seed SOUL.md and registries.
> 2. Capture principles via `/ztn:capture-candidate` over time, or seed
>    a starter set yourself in `0_constitution/axiom/`.
> 3. Run `/ztn:regen-constitution` — this file gets overwritten with
>    the actual harness view of your constitution.
>
> The installer (`integrations/claude-code/install.sh`) symlinks this
> file into `~/.claude/rules/constitution-core.md`. Until you run the
> regen step, the symlink resolves to this stub — engine prompts that
> @-reference it will read these instructions and behave gracefully.

---

For the full tree — including non-core tier-1 principles, rules, and
trade-off resolution — invoke `/check-decision` after seeding your
constitution.
